_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Hypothesis property-based tests on the state machines (P2-CR-B)

steps.description: RED-first: write tests/unit/test_hypothesis_setup.py pinning (a) `import hypothesis` succeeds, (b) [tool.hypothesis] block present in pyproject.toml with `database_file` key, (c) `properties` marker registered in [tool.pytest.ini_options].markers, (d) tests/unit/properties/conftest.py exists and registers the three named profiles (ci, dev, deep). Run it; confirm RED (ImportError on hypothesis is acceptable RED here — capture the test id + the actual failure line for tdd_red_evidence). Then: add `hypothesis>=6.100,<7` to [dependency-groups] dev in pyproject.toml; run `uv lock`; add [tool.hypothesis] block (database_file = '.hypothesis/examples'); register `properties` marker; add `.hypothesis/` to .gitignore; create tests/unit/properties/__init__.py and tests/unit/properties/conftest.py (registers ci max_examples=20 deadline=2000 derandomize=True; dev max_examples=200 deadline=5000; deep max_examples=1000 deadline=None derandomize=False; loads profile from IW_HYPOTHESIS_PROFILE env var defaulting to 'ci'; pytest_collection_modifyitems hook auto-applies `properties` marker to every test in this dir). Write the 5 property modules per design (test_work_item_lifecycle_properties.py with RuleBasedStateMachine + 4 invariants; test_batch_lifecycle_properties.py with 5 pure-function properties; test_fix_cycle_cap_properties.py with RuleBasedStateMachine + cap invariant; test_doc_diff_round_trip_properties.py with two `parse(serialise(d)) == d` round-trip properties; test_iw_next_id_atomicity_properties.py with RuleBasedStateMachine using ThreadPoolExecutor over testcontainer db_session — pulls in tests.integration.conftest via pytest_plugins). If batch-status computation in orch/daemon/batch_manager.py is too DB-coupled to test as a pure function, extract a minimal pure helper `compute_batch_status(items: list[tuple[ItemId, ItemStatus]]) -> BatchStatus`; existing batch tests must still pass after the extraction (sanity-check by running them locally). Add 2 Makefile targets: `test-properties` (sets IW_HYPOTHESIS_PROFILE=ci explicitly, runs the dir with --no-cov) and `test-properties-deep` (sets IW_HYPOTHESIS_PROFILE=deep). Add both to .PHONY. Run `time make test-properties` and `time IW_HYPOTHESIS_PROFILE=dev uv run pytest tests/unit/properties/ -v --no-cov` and capture wall-clock per profile per file into evidences/pre/cr-00060-profile-wall-clock.txt. Re-run RED test → GREEN. Update docs/IW_AI_Core_Testing_Strategy.md: §3 add new 'Property-based tests' sub-section; §5 add 2 rows (ci profile in make test-unit; deep profile on-demand); §9 row 'Property-based tests' flip ❌→✅ with CR number. Update tests/CLAUDE.md: new 'Property tests' sub-section (conventions). Update skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md: new property-tests sub-section in conventions. Run `iw sync-skills --force iw-ai-core-testing`. Update ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md: §6 item 2.2 → DONE; §11 new dated changelog entry. Pre-flight: `make format` + `make typecheck` + `make lint`. Targeted verification: `make test-properties` exits 0, the 5 modules collect under -m properties. Do NOT run `make check` / `make test-integration` / `make diff-coverage` — that's S08/S09/S10's job. 3000s timeout.

Review S01: hypothesis dep installed (>=6.100,<7); [tool.hypothesis] block has database_file key; `properties` marker registered; .gitignore has .hypothesis/; tests/unit/properties/conftest.py registers all three profiles with the named example counts (ci=20 derandomize=True, dev=200, deep=1000) and the marker-auto-apply hook; all 5 property files present; each RuleBasedStateMachine has ≥1 @invariant and ≥2 @rule decorators (CRITICAL on missing invariants); the work-item-lifecycle invariants match the 4 named in the design (terminal-merged-no-transition; fix_cycle ≤ MAX; no-re-claim-of-terminal; current_step_index monotonic); the batch-lifecycle properties match the 5 named (deterministic; held precedence; completed-iff; failed-iff; in_progress otherwise); the doc-diff module uses `assume()` to skip pathological inputs rather than silently passing (HIGH if no assume calls — too-loose strategy); the next-id atomicity test uses the testcontainer db_session fixture (CRITICAL if mocked engine); profile selection via IW_HYPOTHESIS_PROFILE actually works (run `IW_HYPOTHESIS_PROFILE=dev uv run pytest tests/unit/properties/test_work_item_lifecycle_properties.py --hypothesis-show-statistics 2>&1 | grep -i examples` and confirm example count > ci default); the evidences/pre/cr-00060-profile-wall-clock.txt file exists with REAL numbers (not TBD); ci profile total wall-clock < 30s (CRITICAL if > 60s — would push make test-unit over budget); strategy doc §3/§5/§9 + tests/CLAUDE.md + skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md + .claude/skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md all updated (skill master + sync byte-identical; HIGH on drift); §6 item 2.2 = DONE; §11 changelog entry has all 5 module names + 3 profiles + 2 Makefile targets + wall-clock; scope-creep audit: no files outside scope; if orch/daemon/batch_manager.py was edited, the only change is helper extraction (pure refactor — existing tests still pass at S01; CRITICAL on any behaviour change in batch-status computation).

Global cross-agent review: independently run `make test-properties` end-to-end and confirm 0 exit; independently run `IW_HYPOTHESIS_PROFILE=deep uv run pytest tests/unit/properties/ -v --no-cov --timeout=900` (allow 15min for the deep sweep — this is the on-demand profile but worth running once at review time to confirm it doesn't surface a real bug; if it does, flag as a HIGH finding with the shrunk counterexample, recommend filing a separate incident, but do NOT block this CR's merge — finding a bug here is the system working as intended; if deep passes too, that's a strong signal); inspect `pytest tests/unit/properties/ --collect-only -m properties` to confirm all tests are collected under the marker (auto-apply hook works); inspect `pytest tests/unit/properties/ --collect-only -m "not properties"` to confirm 0 tests under that dir are unmarked (HIGH if any straggler); independently verify the cross-doc-square: strategy doc §3 sub-section + §5 rows + §9 row + tests/CLAUDE.md sub-section + skill sub-section name the SAME 5 modules and SAME 3 profiles (drift = HIGH); if batch_manager.py was extracted, independently run the existing batch tests (`uv run pytest tests/integration/test_cli_batches.py -v`) to confirm pure-refactor — same results before and after (CRITICAL on regression). Confirm `make quality` (lint + format-check + typecheck + test-assertions + dead-code + dep-check) passes — particularly: deptry MUST NOT flag hypothesis as unused (it's only used by tests; verify [tool.deptry] config tolerates this OR the hypothesis import is in a non-test file mistakenly). Run `make test-unit` to confirm the new property modules run as part of the gate via the ci profile (S08 will re-run this, but pre-confirm here). Phase-2 cumulative signal: this is the 2nd Phase-2 CR — surface in the report whether the full-setup shape (this CR) vs the spike-then-setup shape (CR-00059) was the right call. Timeout 1800s (deep profile run is the big-ticket item).
QV: Unit tests (now includes 5 new property modules at the ci profile via tests/unit/properties/conftest.py default)

QV: Integration tests (no new integration tests this CR — but the existing suite must still pass; the next-id property test pulling in tests.integration.conftest must not regress integration collection)
QV: Diff coverage (new property tests should cover the touched lines well — if batch_manager.py was edited, the new pure helper should be >=90% covered by the property module)

Self-assessment via iw-item-analyze. Phase-2 second CR — Phase-2-specific findings: (a) did the full-setup shape (5 modules in one CR) work, or would spike-then-setup have been smarter for any module; (b) did any property test surface a real bug during S01's GREEN-running iteration (those are pure value-add, worth calling out); (c) did the deep profile in S03 surface anything the ci profile missed (informs whether ci is too narrow); (d) was the conftest's marker auto-apply hook a clean pattern to add or did reviewers struggle to verify it; (e) is the next-id property test in tests/unit/properties/ (DB-backed but in unit dir) the right call, or should DB-backed properties go in tests/integration/properties/ for future modules (informs Phase-3 contract-sweep CR shape); (f) cumulative Phase-2 cost — CR-00059 + CR-00060 timing combined, to calibrate Phase-2's remaining item 2.3 (P2-CR-C, flaky/quarantine).
